text:
It was a quiet morning just after dawn when I spotted a squirrel climbing upwards a tree trunk outside our kitchen window. Its tail flicked back and forth as it examined something in its hand.

"What are you holding?" I called out.

A tiny nut.

"Why are you collecting those?" I guessed.

Just treasures.

"I collect memories." The squirrel paused for a second. "Not things?"

"No. Just things."

"Why don't you ever forget them?" it asked.

Maybe I will.

I sat down and started drawing.

"The world sure does treasure those moments."

When finished?

"You made your own treasure map!"

I did?

"Oh. Yeah. You'd know nuts."

I just love squirrels.

*Later that day:*

I left an actual nut outside the squirrel's nest. It scurried back enthusiastically.

The forest's full of secrets.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
